contest.title: 2024-2025 ICPC Asia Jakarta Regional Contest (Unrated, Online Mirror, ICPC Rules, Teams Preferred)

sources.notes: Read the full statement and constraints: |S|<=5000; letters may be deleted and rearranged; Y is flexible, and NG consumes two letters while acting as one consonant.

Read the official Jakarta analysis. It groups letters into fixed vowels, ordinary consonants, Y, N, and G, enumerates the numbers of NG units and vowel-Y assignments, and evaluates 3s+min(2s,p) in O(n^2).
Re-derived the resource formula and exhaustively checked it against direct syllable-resource feasibility for all five category counts from 0 through 4.

annotation.summary: Delete and rearrange letters to maximize the total character length of a word made of consonant-vowel-consonant syllables, where Y is flexible and NG may occupy one consonant slot using two characters.
annotation.constraints: 1<=|S|<=5000, one uppercase string. The quadratic enumeration over two bounded count choices fits the 1-second limit.
annotation.core_idea: Count fixed vowels v0, ordinary consonants c0 excluding N/G/Y, and counts nN, nG, nY. Enumerate p, the number of N-G pairs treated as two-letter consonant units, and q, the number of Y letters assigned as vowels. Then V=v0+q and C=c0+nN+nG-p+(nY-q), because each NG pair replaces two single consonant units by one. The number of syllables is s=min(V,floor(C/2)). Among the 2s consonant slots, use NG units before one-letter consonants because each used NG adds one character, giving length 3s+min(p,2s). Maximize this expression over all p and q; zero remains the answer when no syllable is feasible.
annotation.complexity: O(n^2) time and O(1) auxiliary memory.
tricks: Reduce an arbitrary rearrangement to five frequency categories.
Enumerate the two flexible choices: how many NG units to form and how many Y letters become vowels.
For a fixed syllable count, prioritize NG units in consonant slots because each contributes one extra output character.

solution_variants.name: frequency-enumeration
solution_variants.summary: Enumerate the chosen number of NG consonant units and the vowel/consonant split of Y, compute the feasible syllable count from aggregate resources, and greedily fill consonant slots with NG first.
solution_variants.complexity: O(n^2) time and O(1) auxiliary memory.

tags.evidence: The accepted official solution explicitly enumerates every feasible pair (p,q) of NG-unit count and vowel-Y count, then evaluates each state in O(1).
Deletion and arbitrary rearrangement eliminate positional information; feasibility and value depend only on the five category counts and the equations V=v0+q, C=c0+nN+nG-p+nY-q.
After fixing p, q, and s, placing as many NG units as possible into the 2s used consonant slots is always optimal because each replaces a one-letter consonant unit and adds one character.
